content.body: Linear programming finds the best (maximum or minimum) value of an objective function subject to constraints written as linear inequalities.
Inequality signs: < (less than), > (greater than), ≤ (less than or equal), ≥ (greater than or equal)
The feasible region is the set of all points that satisfy ALL constraints simultaneously. It is found by shading and identifying the overlapping region on a graph.

content.title: Setting Up Constraints
content.body:
A factory makes chairs (x) and tables (y).
Constraints: x ≥ 0, y ≥ 0 (can't make negative items)
Labour: 2x + 3y ≤ 12
Material: x + y ≤ 5
Objective: Maximise profit P = 3x + 4y
Steps: 1) Draw each inequality on a graph. 2) Identify the feasible region. 3) Find corner points. 4) Test objective function at each corner. 5) Choose max or min.

questions.question: The feasible region in linear programming satisfies:
questions.explanation: The feasible region is where ALL inequalities are satisfied at the same time.

questions.question: The objective function in linear programming is:
options: A function to be maximised or minimised
questions.answer: A function to be maximised or minimised
questions.explanation: The objective function (like profit or cost) is what we want to optimise.

questions.question: x ≥ 0 and y ≥ 0 means the solution must be in:
questions.explanation: x ≥ 0 means right side, y ≥ 0 means upper side — this is the first quadrant.

questions.question: The maximum/minimum of the objective function occurs at:
options: The centre of the feasible region
A corner point of the feasible region
questions.answer: A corner point of the feasible region
questions.explanation: The optimal value always occurs at a vertex (corner point) of the feasible region.

questions.question: Constraints 2x + y ≤ 10 and x + 2y ≤ 10 with x,y ≥ 0. Corner points include the origin (0,0) and:
Objective function P = 5x + 3y. At (0,0): P=0, at (4,0): P=20, at (0,6): P=18. Maximum is:
questions.explanation: Test each corner: highest value is P=20 at (4,0).

questions.question: The line 3x + 2y = 12 has x-intercept:
questions.explanation: Set y=0: 3x=12, x=4. x-intercept is (4,0).

questions.question: For minimisation problems in linear programming, the optimal value is found at:
options: The corner point with minimum objective function value
questions.answer: The corner point with minimum objective function value
questions.explanation: Just like maximisation, but we choose the corner with the LOWEST value.

questions.question: x + y ≤ 6, x ≥ 1, y ≥ 2. Which point is in the feasible region?
questions.explanation: Check (1,2): 1+2=3≤6✓, 1≥1✓, 2≥2✓. All constraints satisfied.

questions.question: A farmer plants maize (x) and beans (y). Constraint: x + y ≤ 100. This means:
questions.explanation: ≤ means 'less than or equal to' — total land used cannot exceed 100 acres.

questions.question: The number of corner points tested equals:
options: The number of vertices of the feasible region
questions.answer: The number of vertices of the feasible region
questions.explanation: We test the objective function at every corner (vertex) of the feasible polygon.

questions.question: Maximise P = 2x + y subject to x + y ≤ 4, x ≥ 0, y ≥ 0. Corners: (0,0),(4,0),(0,4). Max P =
Which point satisfies 2x + 3y ≤ 12 AND x + y ≤ 5?
questions.explanation: (2,2): 2(2)+3(2)=10≤12✓, 2+2=4≤5✓. All others fail one constraint.

questions.question: In linear programming, 'non-negativity constraints' mean:
options: Variables must be zero or positive
questions.answer: Variables must be zero or positive
questions.explanation: x ≥ 0 and y ≥ 0 are non-negativity constraints — real quantities can't be negative.

questions.question: A business minimises cost C = 3x + 5y. At corners: (2,4)→26, (0,6)→30, (4,2)→22. Minimum cost =
questions.explanation: Minimum is the lowest value = 22 at corner (4,2).

questions.question: The y-intercept of 4x + 5y = 20 is:
questions.explanation: Set x=0: 5y=20, y=4. y-intercept is (0,4).

questions.question: Feasible region with corners (0,0),(6,0),(0,4). Maximise P=x+2y. Best corner is:
The intersection of x + y = 8 and 2x + y = 12 is:
questions.explanation: Subtract: x=4. Then y=4.

questions.question: Linear programming is used in real life for:
questions.explanation: It optimises use of limited resources (labour, materials, land) to maximise profit or minimise cost.

questions.question: If the feasible region is empty (no points satisfy all constraints), the linear program has:
questions.explanation: An empty feasible region means the constraints are contradictory — no solution exists.
